name: Spec Aligner
rationale: Existing compiler gate lacks spec-to-release traceability.

name: PR Narrator
rationale: Narration is covered only as a contract artifact.

name: Review Router
rationale: Router and progressive disclosure are explicit in MAD-SKILL-008.

name: Decision Log
rationale: Decision and feedback lifecycle are covered by the rubric-safe loop.

name: Issue Gardener
rationale: Traceability is missing, not a separate skill identity.

name: Design Sync
rationale: The missing part is review evidence, not a design runtime.

name: Security Buddy
rationale: Threat and permission review are already core compiler evidence.

name: Knowledge Base
rationale: Authorized source-to-skill handling is already covered.

name: Onboarding Map
rationale: Decision-to-diagram covers structural orientation.

name: Retrospective Bot
rationale: Feedback and retained procedure evidence are covered.

name: Agent Swarm
rationale: Control-plane separation rejects unbounded agent execution.

name: Playwright Scout
rationale: Existing demonstration gate is not browser QA.

name: Terminal Sense
rationale: Capability contract covers command evidence and side effects.

name: CI Fixer
rationale: Fixing remains an approved implementation action, not implicit automation.

name: Release Notes
rationale: A traceability chain is needed before publication.

name: Data Cleaner
rationale: Data actions require explicit contracts and output checks.

name: Screenshot QA
rationale: Existing privacy gate does not define visual QA acceptance.

name: Changelog Miner
rationale: Retrospective evidence already covers historical procedure signals.

name: Dependency Guard
rationale: Dependencies remain permissioned and testable under contracts.

name: Nightly Runner
rationale: No current experiment owns scheduling, budgets, cancellation, or circuit breaking.

name: Context Pack
rationale: Planning and handoff are already bounded source artifacts.

name: Repo Cartographer
rationale: Safe project maps are the stated MAD-SKILL-001 scope.

name: Test Pilot
rationale: Real-output harness is the required evaluator boundary.

name: Debug Radar
rationale: Debugging evidence belongs to the contract harness.

name: Refactor Lens
rationale: Refactors need the same output and regression evidence.

name: API Stitcher
rationale: API work is not separate from capability and rollback contracts.

name: Migration Buddy
rationale: Migration validation is a contract-harness concern.

name: Docs Whisperer
rationale: Traceability adds stale and orphan detection.

name: Prompt Harness
rationale: Prompt claims need baseline and output evidence.

name: Ship Checklist
rationale: Readiness must be evidenced rather than checklist-only.

name: Planning with Files
rationale: Use bounded records with secret exclusions and compaction.

name: Superpowers
rationale: Composable routing is covered; source remains unverified.

name: Code Review
rationale: Review is a serial role, not an autonomous approval tier.

name: Webapp Testing
rationale: A new candidate can define offline planning and evidence without browser action.

name: Code Simplifier
rationale: Simplification needs regression and output validation.

name: UI UX Pro Max
rationale: A concept name is not a verifiable source or safe runtime proposal.

name: MCP Builder
rationale: MCP creation and registration require independent high-risk approvals.

name: Ralph Loop
rationale: Only the pause-and-handoff method is relevant; source remains ambiguous.

name: PPTX
rationale: Planning maps to media separation; generation stays isolated.

name: Skill Creator
rationale: Skill selection and composition are router responsibilities.
